arabic: زَارَ الشَّاعِرُ الشَّيْخُ أُسْتَاذَهُ الْقَدِيمَ بَعْدَ سَنَوَاتٍ طَوِيلَةٍ مِنَ الْغِيَابِ. وَجَدَهُ جَالِسًا فِي غُرْفَتِهِ بَيْنَ كُتُبِهِ الْكَثِيرَةِ كَعَادَتِهِ. قَالَ لَهُ الأُسْتَاذُ: «أَنَا فَخُورٌ بِكَ، لَقَدْ أَصْبَحَتَ شَاعِرًا حَقِيقِيًّا.» دَمَعَتْ عَيْنَا الشَّاعِرِ وَتَذَكَّرَ أَوَّلَ قَصِيدَةٍ كَتَبَهَا وَهُوَ طَالِبٌ صَغِيرٌ. قَالَ فِي نَفْسِهِ إِنَّ أَعْظَمَ هَدِيَّةٍ فِي الْحَيَاةِ هِيَ مُعَلِّمٌ يُؤْمِنُ بِكَ.
translation: Stari pjesnik posjetio je svog nekadašnjeg učitelja nakon dugih godina odsustva. Zatekao ga je kako sjedi u sobi okružen gomilom knjiga, kao i uvijek. Učitelj mu je rekao: „Ponosan sam na tebe, postao si pravi pjesnik.
translationEn: The elderly poet visited his old teacher after many years of absence. He found him sitting in his room surrounded by piles of books, as always. The teacher said to him: "I am proud of you — you have become a true poet." The poet's eyes filled with tears and he remembered the first poem he had written as a young student. He told himself that the greatest gift in life is a teacher who believes in you.
